conclusion: The authors report the synthesis and discovery of superconductivity in the hexagonal close-packed (hcp) $P6_3/mmc$ phase of yttrium nonahydride ($YH_{9}$). This phase exhibits a maximum superconducting critical temperature $T_{c}$ of $\sim 243$ K at a pressure of 201 GPa, which represents the highest $T_{c}$ observed in the yttrium-hydrogen system in this study. The pressure dependence of $T_{c}$ forms a dome-like shape, with a sharp drop in $T_{c}$ below $\sim 195$ GPa attributed to structural instability.
